description_long: This kiosk is a semi-circular urban display installation featuring a raised brick planter at its center, filled with a large, stylized purple flower and surrounded by green foliage. Hovering above the flower is a detailed model of a bee, accompanied by small informational placards. The kiosk is set on a concrete platform with patches of grass at the edges, and is flanked by two prominent signposts with multiple directional signs and informational panels. The signs appear to provide educational content, likely about pollinators or local flora. The overall design is open and accessible, inviting passersby to stop, read, and observe the central display.
description: A semi-circular urban kiosk with a central brick planter, a large purple flower, a bee model, and educational signposts on a concrete base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central flower and bee display, both signposts, and the main informational panels clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the depth of the planter, the bee above the flower, and the rear of the left signpost.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the brick planter, the bee, and the backs of both signposts, with less visibility of the informational panels.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the right signpost, the edge of the concrete base, and the grass patches, with partial visibility of the flower display.
attribute_signage_type: Printed signs and informational panels mounted on vertical posts, with directional arrows and educational content; signage is placed at the front and sides of the kiosk.
attribute_roof_design: none; open-air design with no visible roof structure.
attribute_architectural_style: Modern educational display with utilitarian and naturalistic influences.
